goal:
Build an interactive, deployed crypto market dashboard web app.
1. Research and select free public APIs for real-time crypto prices (BTC, ETH, SOL) and Fear & Greed index.
2. Scaffold a React + Tailwind CSS project.
3. Implement the dashboard UI (price cards, line chart, Fear & Greed index).
4. Integrate API fetching logic with error handling.
5. Test the application locally.
6. Deploy the application to a free hosting service (e.g., Vercel).
7. Create a README file for API key management.

steps.name: Research APIs
steps.description: Use web_search to identify free, no-auth-required endpoints for CoinGecko or Binance for BTC, ETH, SOL prices and the Alternative.me API for the Fear & Greed Index.
steps.done_when: API endpoints for price data and Fear & Greed index are identified and documented.

steps.name: Scaffold Project
steps.description: Run shell commands to create a new React project with Vite and install Tailwind CSS and Recharts for data visualization.
steps.done_when: Project directory exists with React, Tailwind, and Recharts dependencies installed.

steps.name: Create API Service
steps.description: Create src/services/cryptoApi.js to handle fetch requests for the identified endpoints with error handling.
steps.done_when: src/services/cryptoApi.js is created and exports functional fetch methods.

steps.name: Implement Dashboard UI
steps.description: Write src/App.jsx using Tailwind CSS to create a responsive grid layout containing price cards and a Recharts line chart component.
steps.done_when: App.jsx is written with layout, cards, and chart placeholders.

steps.name: Integrate Logic
steps.description: Edit src/App.jsx to import the API service and implement React state hooks to display live data.
steps.done_when: App.jsx fetches and renders real-time data from the APIs.

steps.name: Local Verification
steps.description: Run the development server using system_shell to verify the dashboard renders correctly and handles API errors.
steps.done_when: Application runs without console errors and displays data correctly.

steps.name: Create Documentation
steps.description: Create a README.md file explaining how to run the app and manage environment variables for API keys if needed.
steps.done_when: README.md is created in the root directory.

steps.name: Deploy to Vercel
steps.description: Use system_shell to initialize a git repository and run the Vercel CLI deployment command.
steps.done_when: The application is live on a Vercel URL.
